title: Querying Deep Dive
description: Master query operators, logical conditions, array queries, and projections.

sections.heading: Comparison operators — SQL vs MongoDB
AND, OR, NOT, NOR

sections.content: **Performance tip:** Put the most selective filter first in an `$or` array — MongoDB evaluates them left to right and can short-circuit. Same advice applies in PostgreSQL WHERE clauses.
Querying arrays in MongoDB is one of the biggest advantages over SQL. You can search inside arrays without joins or subqueries.

sections.heading: Querying array fields
Array queries — the SQL headache vs MongoDB ease
table: SELECT b.* FROM books b JOIN book_genres bg ON b.id = bg.book_id WHERE bg.genre = 'fiction'
... WHERE bg.genre IN ('fiction', 'classic') GROUP BY b.id HAVING COUNT(*) = 2

sections.heading: Field existence and type checks

sections.content: **Regex performance:** Like SQL `LIKE '%text%'`, a leading wildcard regex (`/text/`) cannot use an index. Anchored patterns (`/^text/`) can. Use MongoDB Atlas Search for full production text search.
**The null vs $exists trap.** This is one of the most common MongoDB gotchas. Querying `{ field: null }` and `{ field: { $exists: false } }` look similar but behave differently.

sections.heading: null vs $exists — not the same thing

sections.content: **Index selectivity** means how effectively an index narrows down results. A highly selective index matches very few documents for a given query value — so MongoDB does less work. A low-selectivity index matches many documents and may not help much.

sections.heading: High vs low selectivity examples

sections.content: **Practical rule:** Index fields that have many distinct values (email, username, orderId). Be cautious indexing boolean or low-cardinality fields — MongoDB's query planner may ignore the index and do a collection scan instead, which can actually be faster.
**$nor — neither condition is true.** `$nor` is the opposite of `$or`. It returns documents where ALL the listed conditions are false.

sections.heading: $nor example
